vulnerabilities.description: strcpy copies src into dst without any length check; the caller is entirely responsible for ensuring dst is large enough, making this function inherently unsafe to use.

cross_function.description: handle_request reads up to 255 bytes from a file descriptor and passes the full string to process_input, which has only a 64-byte local_buf; unsafe_copy performs strcpy without bounds checking, so any input longer than 63 bytes overflows the stack buffer.
